title: Associate Director Corporate Development
description:
At Zimmer Biomet, we believe in pushing the boundaries of innovation and driving our mission forward. As a global medical technology leader for nearly 100 years, a patient’s mobility is enhanced by a Zimmer Biomet product or technology every 8 seconds.

As a Zimmer Biomet team member, you will share in our commitment to providing mobility and renewed life to people around the world. To support our talent team, we focus on development opportunities, robust employee resource groups (ERGs), a flexible working environment, location specific competitive total rewards, wellness incentives and a culture of recognition and performance awards. We are committed to creating an environment where every team member feels included, respected, empowered and recognised.


What You Can Expect

Lead and support the sourcing, evaluation, and execution of mergers & acquisitions, investments, and strategic partnership opportunities in support of Zimmer Biomet’s inorganic growth strategy. Serve as a key contributor across the full transaction lifecycle, including opportunity assessment, valuation and business case development, due diligence leadership, transaction execution, and post-transaction planning. Partner closely with Corporate Development leadership, business unit leaders, Corporate Strategy, and cross-functional stakeholders to advance high-quality deal flow and support informed strategic decision-making. Build and maintain relationships with external advisors, bankers, and other intermediaries to support efficient execution and opportunity development.

How You'll Create Impact
Lead financial modeling, valuation, and business case development for M&A, investment, and partnership opportunities.
Drive key aspects of transaction evaluation, including strategic fit assessment, market analysis, competitive review, and risk identification.
Lead diligence workstreams across finance, legal, tax, strategy, regulatory, and business unit teams to support efficient and thorough evaluation of opportunities.
Partner with business units and Corporate Strategy to prioritize opportunities aligned to Zimmer Biomet’s growth objectives and portfolio strategy.
Prepare clear, concise, and executive-ready materials, including transaction summaries, investment memos, presentations, and recommendations for senior leadership.
Support transaction execution activities, including process management, issue resolution, timeline tracking, advisor coordination, and document review.
Contribute to deal structuring discussions and negotiation preparation by evaluating strategic, financial, and operational tradeoffs.
Build and maintain relationships with investment bankers, external advisors, and other intermediaries to support a strong pipeline of relevant opportunities.
Help guide post-transaction planning activities, including integration readiness, milestone tracking, and performance follow-up as assigned.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of Corporate Development processes, templates, tools, and analytical frameworks to drive consistency and efficiency across transactions.
Mentor less experienced team members and provide guidance on modeling, research, and transaction process execution.
What Makes You Stand Out
Strong experience in M&A, investments, strategic partnerships, and/or corporate development, ideally within MedTech, medical devices, healthcare, or life sciences.
Strong understanding of valuation, transaction economics, business case development, and diligence workflows.
Ability to independently lead complex analyses and manage multiple transaction workstreams simultaneously.
Strong strategic thinking and ability to connect market trends, competitive dynamics, and portfolio priorities to opportunity assessment.
Effective cross-functional leadership skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and drive alignment in a matrixed environment.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to distill complex issues into clear reco
... (truncated to 4000 characters)

title: Lead Automation & Robotics Engineer – (ABB & RAPID Systems Expert)
description:
Job Function

Lead Automation & Robotics Engineer – (ABB & RAPID Systems Expert)

Up to $120,000 + Dynamic Bonuses


Waukesha, WI Area
Elite 1st Shift & Cutting-Edge R&D Join a premier, certified custom systems integrator where your engineering concepts are built right on the shop floor Work in a highly creative, tech-driven environment backed by a family-first culture that prioritizes true work-life balance
EXPERIENCE 5+ Years of Custom Industrial Automation & Advanced ABB Robotics Integration
SOFTWARE Mastery of ABB RobotStudio, RAPID Programming, and Allen-Bradley PLC Systems
SKILLS From-Scratch System Design, Motion Control, Machine Safety, and Full-Scale Commissioning
COMP Up to $120,000 Base Salary (Based on technical depth) + Full Corporate Benefits Package
TRAVEL High-Autonomy Role with up to 30% Domestic and International Travel for Client Commissioning
LOCATION Greater Waukesha, WI area (100% Onsite with shop-floor integration; No Remote)
AUTH US Citizen or Green Card Holder required


If you are an elite robotics professional feeling stifled by repetitive, high-volume manufacturing lines or rigid corporate structures, this is the career destination you have been looking for. Our client is a rapidly growing, certified custom industrial automation systems integrator. They don't do cookie-cutter projects; they engineer custom, first-of-their-kind automated systems for a highly diversified global customer base. Here, creativity and continuous learning aren't just buzzwords—they are backed by substantial, hands-on R&D budgets.

This company is explicitly built on an employee-centric, family-oriented culture where engineers are respected as technical innovators, not just numbers. Management actively protects your work-life balance, and because the engineering offices sit under the same roof as production, you get the unique professional satisfaction of watching your digital simulations come to life on the shop floor. For a programmer with a passion for bleeding-edge tech, this role offers zero boredom, high-stakes technical ownership, and an incredibly stable, collaborative career home.

What You Will Be Doing


Developing, simulating, and optimizing complex robotic programs from scratch using ABB RobotStudio and RAPID code structures
Owning the full project lifecycle, seamlessly bridging the gap between initial 3D digital simulation, testing, debugging, and live field commissioning
Configuring initial robot setups, spatial coordinate systems, payload settings, and integrating multi-axis systems with complex PLC networks
Serving as the Lead Technical SME to troubleshoot and resolve intricate robotic logic, motion control, and communication interface errors
Partnering closely with cross-functional mechanical and controls teams to design custom end-of-arm tooling (EOAT) and machine safety parameters
Traveling up to 30% annually (both domestically and internationally) to manage high-visibility on-site installations, final line integration, and customer handoffs
Generating and maintaining meticulous system documentation, wiring schematics, and functional program architectures


What We Are Looking For


ABB Domain Expertise: 5 or more years of dedicated, hands-on experience programming and integrating ABB robotic arms within complex, custom automation settings
Advanced Coding: 2+ years of robust, fluid proficiency utilizing ABB RobotStudio for virtual simulations and writing native RAPID code from scratch
Controls Integration: 2+ years of proven experience interfacing robotics with industrial PLC systems (Allen-Bradley Studio 5000 / RSLogix preferred)
Hardware & Safety: 2+ years of deep technical familiarity with motion control hardware, vision systems, fieldbus networks (EtherNet/IP, PROFINET), and safety protocols
Road Warrior Ready: Complete willingness and valid credentials to travel up to 30% internationally and domestically for client startups
Stability: A reliable, rock-solid professional history showing consecutive te
... (truncated to 4000 characters)

title: Project Development Engineer, Tesla Energy
description:
What To Expect
As a Project Development Engineer, you will serve as a key technical member of a pre-construction team. Your primary focus will be evaluating and implementing strategies for battery energy storage, solar, and microgrid projects utilizing Tesla technology. You will manage the technical development of diverse project portfolios, oversee engineering activities to minimize risks, and handle a mix of large-scale utility projects alongside smaller, customized initiatives.

The Energy Projects team is a cross-functional group responsible for the engineering and execution of solar and storage projects. This includes development, design, analysis, project management, estimation, construction, and commissioning. Positioned centrally within business development, Tesla technology, and project deployment across the Americas, team members drive success across a wide range of applications.

This role requires a passion for transforming energy infrastructure in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment. Expect a rock-solid work ethic, team orientation, creativity, and passion for innovation in a dynamic tech landscape.

What You'll Do


Own project development and execution for Tesla EPC projects, defining scope, responsibilities, and BOP specifications with Tesla products
Deliver technical support for proposals including custom drawings, diagrams, layouts, specifications reviews
Identify and communicate scope gaps, risks, and mitigation strategies; drawing on engineering, construction, interconnection, and permitting knowledge
Assess project sites, advance interconnection studies, and pursue permitting as needed



What You'll Bring


Degree in Engineering or related field, or equivalent in experience
2+ years’ experience in design & construction of power generation projects, emphasizing MV/HV substations (experience in utility-scale PV or battery storage preferred)
Expertise in electrical engineering, including power, controls, communications, fire protection, engineering calculations, CAD drafting & design, standards and code compliance
Skills in contracting: identifying and negotiating terms, developing scopes and division of responsibility docs
Strong written and verbal communications, and ability to provide technical guidance
Experience in fast-paced organizations with ability to prioritize multiple projects or tasks



Benefits
Compensation and Benefits
Along with competitive pay, as a full-time Tesla employee, you are eligible for the following benefits at day 1 of hire:

Medical plans > plan options with $0 payroll deduction
Family-building, fertility, adoption and surrogacy benefits
Dental (including orthodontic coverage) and vision plans, both have options with a $0 paycheck contribution
Company Paid (Health Savings Accounts) HSA Contribution when enrolled in the High-Deductible medical plan with HSA
Healthcare and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA)
401(k) with employer match, Employee Stock Purchase Plans, and other financial benefits
Company paid Basic Life, AD&D
Short-term and long-term disability insurance (90 day waiting period)
Employee Assistance Program
Sick and Vacation time (Flex time for salary positions, Accrued hours for Hourly positions), and Paid Holidays
Back-up childcare and parenting support resources
Voluntary benefits to include: critical illness, hospital indemnity, accident insurance, theft & legal services, and pet insurance
Weight Loss and Tobacco Cessation Programs
Tesla Babies program
Commuter benefits
Employee discounts and perks program



, Tesla
